summary: End-to-end regression of Yigim payment migration + entitlement-grant bug fix. All 8 entitlement tests pass and all 23 Yigim API integration tests pass. Frontend smoke covers all listed routes including /products, /payment-success, /payment-failed without crashes.

minor.issue: Yigim sandbox returns 'Invalid merchant name' because YIGIM_MERCHANT='Merchant' is a placeholder. Backend handles this gracefully with HTTP 500 + {detail: ...}, but no yigim_transactions row is persisted on failure (only on upstream success). Per agent note this is a deployment-time concern, not a code bug. Consider inserting the pending transaction BEFORE calling Yigim so failed attempts are auditable.

integration_issues.issue: When the user is unauthenticated, the page short-circuits to the login screen (because the 2s polling endpoint /api/yigim/transaction requires auth). After Yigim redirects the user back, the SPA session may have expired, leading to a confusing UX. Consider relaxing /api/yigim/transaction/{order_id} to lookup-by-reference without auth or caching session until callback completes.

issues: React warning: 'React does not recognize the backgroundColor prop on a DOM element' fires twice on initial load. Some component is spreading a camelCase prop onto a native DOM element (likely a styled wrapper). Cosmetic but noisy in prod logs.

action_items: Update YIGIM_MERCHANT/YIGIM_BILLER in /app/backend/.env to a real sandbox merchant name to enable end-to-end Yigim checkout (deployment task).
Fix the React 'backgroundColor' DOM-prop warning (find component spreading style props onto a div).
Optional: persist yigim_transactions with status='pending' BEFORE calling Yigim, so failed upstream calls are auditable.
Optional: rework /payment-success polling so it does not silently redirect unauthenticated users to login after a real Yigim redirect.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 2895 lines — split per-feature routers (yigim, auth, courses, signals, arbitrage) into modules under /app/backend/routers/ to stay <700 lines per file.
server.py uses deprecated @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown'); migrate to FastAPI lifespan handlers.

grant_yigim_entitlement uses {'$set': {flag: True, ...}} via update_one — verified idempotent, but consider returning the updated user document for callers to use instead of a bool, to avoid an extra find_one round trip elsewhere.
yigim/callback always redirects pending payments to /payment-success which then polls — fine, but rate-limit / cap polling on the frontend so it does not loop forever for abandoned carts.

success_rate.frontend: 100% smoke (12/12 routes render, products page shows 4 products with correct USD prices)

seed_data_creation: None persisted — entitlement tests insert+cleanup their own users/transactions; API tests only POST checkout which fails upstream so no rows are created.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run `cd /app/backend && python -m pytest tests/test_yigim_entitlement.py tests/test_yigim_api.py -v` for full Yigim regression. The sandbox merchant placeholder will keep checkout/create returning 500 — that is expected and the test allows it. To exercise a real end-to-end checkout, swap YIGIM_MERCHANT/YIGIM_BILLER in /app/backend/.env with valid sandbox creds and re-run.
rca_of_the_issue: Original recurring entitlement bug: prior payment-callback handler updated yigim_transactions but never wrote *_access flags onto the user. The fix introduces grant_yigim_entitlement(transaction) which (a) flips course_access / book_access / signals_subscription / arbitrage_subscription based on product_type, (b) sets a 30-day expiry on subscription products, (c) inserts a purchases record with payment_method='yigim' and yigim_reference, and (d) is idempotent via a guard that returns False if a purchase row with the same yigim_reference already exists. Verified by 8 mocked tests covering all 4 product types, idempotency, approved/declined/pending status transitions, and the negative path (declined never grants access).
